red.scratchpad:
- Add retreat_until attribute to all units that may need to commit to retreat (mbt, infantry, mortar, medic, drone). Prevent AttributeError crashes.
- Prioritize killing enemy medics and mbt first; then low‑HP units; retreat when health <30% and commit for 8 ticks.
- Mortar should stay on cover line x=22 (or x=41) and use line_of_sight to fire over central wall; retreat to cover if health low.
- Infantry should stay near x=22 cover, use mbt as shield, and follow the same priority/retreat logic.
- Medic must stay with infantry, heal allies below 50% HP, and avoid moving into enemy melee range.
- Drone should orbit the edge of its 18‑tile vision, broadcasting enemy positions each tick; attack only when an enemy is within 2 tiles.
- Keep a balanced point budget (≈89 pts) to maintain durability while preserving firepower.

blue.scratchpad: Enemy massed infantry (18) swamped our front line; mbt tanks survived but were overwhelmed without cover or flanking infantry. Mortars excelled when behind x=22 or x=41 but died quickly when exposed — enforce cover positioning and step back after each shot. Medics fell early; keep them shielded by infantry and heal the most wounded ally each tick. Drones have high vision but die in melee; use them to broadcast enemy positions each tick and harass low‑HP targets, avoid getting into close combat. Commit to attacks by caching target id and using retreat_until when hp <30%; honor the retreat timer to prevent oscillating. Use world.path to navigate around obstacles instead of walking into walls. Prioritize killing enemy medics and low‑HP infantry first.
